name: Neutron-star mass-radius relation (NICER + GW170817)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the observed neutron-star mass-radius relation as constrained by NICER X-ray pulse-profile measurements of PSR J0740+6620 (M = 2.08 +/- 0.07 M_sun (Fonseca et al. 2021), R = 12.39 +1.30/-0.98 km (Miller et al. 2021)) and PSR J0030+0451, plus the GW170817 tidal-deformability constraint. NS exist with masses up to ~2.1 M_sun and radii ~11-13 km; this constrains the dense-matter equation of state and any modified strong-field gravity.

regime.description: Compact-object-scale strong-field gravity, ~ 10 km radius, density up to a few times nuclear saturation density.

current_bound.value: M_max > 2.08 +/- 0.07 M_sun (PSR J0740+6620, Fonseca et al. 2021 refined; supersedes Cromartie et al. 2020 value of 2.14 +0.10/-0.09); R(1.4 M_sun) ~ 11-13 km from joint NICER + GW170817 analyses
current_bound.reference: Fonseca et al., 'Refined Mass and Geometric Measurements of the High-mass PSR J0740+6620', ApJL 915, L12 (2021), arXiv:2104.00880; Miller et al., 'The Radius of PSR J0740+6620 from NICER and XMM-Newton Data', ApJL 918, L28 (2021), arXiv:2105.06979; Riley et al., ApJL 918, L27 (2021); Cromartie et al., Nature Astronomy 4, 72 (2020) (original J0740 mass)

what_it_excludes: Constrains both the dense-matter EOS (must support at least a 2.08 M_sun NS) and modifications to gravity in the strong-field regime. Many scalar-tensor and f(R) gravity models predict NS structure substantially different from GR; the observed M-R region constrains them. Combined GW + NICER data exclude strong scalarization in massive NS.

parameterization.name: Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff (TOV) equation in GR; modified-TOV in alternative gravity. EOS parameterized by piecewise polytropes or by sound-speed parameterization c_s^2(rho).
parameterization.reference: Lattimer and Prakash, Phys. Rep. 442, 109 (2007); Hinderer, ApJ 677, 1216 (2008) (tidal deformability).
parameterization.summary: Given EOS p(rho), TOV gives M-R relation. Modified gravity changes both the TOV structure equations and the mass-radius mapping.

procedural.predicted_observable: Maximum mass M_max; radius at fiducial mass R(1.4 M_sun); tidal deformability Lambda(M).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: M_max > 2.08 M_sun (PSR J0740+6620, Fonseca 2021 refined); Lambda(1.4 M_sun) < 720 (GW170817).
framework_inputs: Modified TOV-like structure equations from the framework's gravity sector.
Dense-matter EOS - typically taken from nuclear physics, but a candidate framework with modified strong interactions must specify how it changes.

primary_references.citation: Fonseca et al., 'Refined Mass and Geometric Measurements of the High-mass PSR J0740+6620', ApJL 915, L12 (2021)
Miller et al., 'The Radius of PSR J0740+6620 from NICER and XMM-Newton Data', ApJL 918, L28 (2021)
Abbott et al. (LVC), 'GW170817: Measurements of Neutron Star Radii and Equation of State', PRL 121, 161101 (2018)
Cromartie et al., 'Relativistic Shapiro delay measurements of an extremely massive millisecond pulsar', Nature Astronomy 4, 72 (2020) (original J0740 mass; superseded by Fonseca 2021)
